title: Hecke Algebras Via Morse Theory of Loop Spaces
speaker: Roman Krutowski
abstract:
Higher-dimensional Heegaard Floer homology (HDHF) is defined by extending Lipshitz's cylindrical reformulation of Heegaard Floer homology from surfaces to arbitrary Liouville domains. The HDHF also serves as a model for Lagrangian Floer homology of symmetric products.
In this talk, I will present a Morse-theoretic model allowing for computations of the HDHF A∞-algebra of k cotangent fibers in the cotangent bundle of a smooth manifold. We apply this model to get an explicit computation of this A∞-algebra for the cotangent bundle of the 2-dimensional sphere. The result of this computation produces a differential graded algebra which may be regarded as the derived HOMFLYPT skein algebra of the sphere.

proof_stages.label: Braid skein algebra target
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines the Morton-Samuelson braid skein algebra of a punctured orientable surface by HOMFLYPT and puncture skein relations, explains product by braid composition and closure to ordinary skein algebras, and identifies the torus case with familiar Hecke-type algebras.

proof_stages.label: Symplectic realization program and K(pi,1) benchmark
proof_stages.summary: The speaker formulates the goal of recovering braid skein algebras from symplectic/Floer theory, situates it among symplectic constructions of quantum-topological invariants, and recalls the earlier theorem identifying higher-dimensional Floer homology of cotangent fibers with the braid skein algebra for K(pi,1) surfaces.

proof_stages.label: Sphere theorem and derived Hecke DGA
proof_stages.summary: For the non-K(pi,1) case of the sphere, the new result is stated as an equivalence with a DGA h_k generated by braid generators t_i in degree 0 and an equatorial generator x_1 in degree -1, whose H_0 recovers the c=1 sphere braid skein algebra while retaining extra derived information.

proof_stages.label: Higher-dimensional Floer algebra from branched covers
proof_stages.summary: The relevant A_infinity Floer algebra is built from Hamiltonian chords between k cotangent fibers with possible endpoint permutations, with operations defined by counts of curves over k-fold simply branched covers of punctured disks and weighted by powers of the formal variable hbar.

proof_stages.label: Path-space Morse model
proof_stages.summary: To compute the cotangent-bundle Floer theory, the talk recalls Morse theory for the Lagrangian action on Sobolev path spaces using Abbondandolo-Schwarz pseudo-gradient fields, including the smoothness of unstable manifolds needed for intersections.

proof_stages.label: Switches as Morse branch points
proof_stages.summary: The Morse model is deformed by diagonal-hit submanifolds and switching maps that exchange tails of intersecting paths, and counts of Morse flow lines and trees with such switches define an A_infinity algebra whose switch count matches the branch-point contribution.

proof_stages.label: Floer-Morse comparison equivalence
proof_stages.summary: An A_infinity equivalence from the branched-cover Floer algebra to the switch-deformed Morse algebra is constructed by counts of half-disks or caps attached to Morse trees, with the key step being the ability to trade holomorphic branch points for Morse switches and invert the deformation from the hbar=0 case.

proof_stages.label: Sphere presentation computation
proof_stages.summary: Using the Morse switch model on S^2, the speaker sketches how the differential of x_1 and the resulting braid-skein relation arise from hemispherical unstable manifolds and local braid resolution, and notes that proving the presentation also requires showing higher A_infinity operations vanish via suitable metrics.
